An amount of $5.00 and $10.00 would be beneficial when signing up for trials subscriptions or streaming services.

title: Extremely Frustrating and Unprofessional Customer Service
review:
I recently received a replacement Citi Costco Visa card. After activating it, I tried to add it to my Apple Pay — but it simply wouldn’t go through. I contacted Citi customer service three separate times, speaking with three different representatives, and none of them were able to resolve the issue.

Each call dragged on with long hold times and unnecessary “security checks,” even after I provided all requested information multiple times. Despite more than an hour combined on the phone, the problem is still unresolved. What’s most frustrating is that I never had this issue with my previous card, so something clearly changed on Citi’s end.

As a loyal Costco member for nearly 40 years with an excellent 850 credit score, I expected a much higher level of professionalism and technical competence. Instead, I experienced confusion, lack of training, and poor communication from every representative I spoke with.

Citibank needs to seriously review and improve its customer service training. Representatives should be knowledgeable, courteous, and able to handle basic issues promptly. This was one of the most disappointing customer service experiences I’ve ever had — enough to make me reconsider using this card altogether.

title: Find another credit card company
review:
I’ve had a really disappointing experience. I never received any alerts about a payment issue until two days after it failed — and it was barely over $100. Then, three days later, I find out the payment was returned with no notice and I was charged both a return fee and an interest fee.

I check my cards daily, and there were no app notifications or emails until five days past the due date saying my payment was late. That’s unacceptable. To make things right, I paid off the full balance immediately — over $1,300 — yet when I contacted support to request a courtesy waiver for the fees, I was told they couldn’t do anything.

That’s too bad, because I have a $9,700 purchase coming up this week, and it’ll be going on my other daily-use credit card (you know, the one from A–Z). They give the same 2% cashback and better service.

This card is only really good for gas cash back at Costco, but with all the options out there now, even that’s not enough to justify the hassle. Chase treats customers better.

title: Frequent Forced Unnecessary Updates!

It seems that about once a month, I am forced to update for “bug fixes and performance improvements”. 

If the app is working fine for me, why must I update it? Updates typically cause the app to use more flash memory every time, and my phone only has 64GB, so I don’t appreciate the bloating! 

The only valid reason for forcing updates is to fix security flaws; otherwise, “If it ain’t broke, DON’T FIX IT!”

title: Terrible banking app
review:
This app is absolutely terrible. My card was locked after making a simple Zelle transfer to my own account. I called support, and after more than an hour of back and forth, they finally unlocked it in the morning. But by the afternoon, it locked again—this time claiming there were two “suspicious” Zelle transactions. My account was frozen once more.

To make matters worse, the app asked me for my full bank account number, which I could only get by either logging into the app (which was locked!) or calling support again. I ended up stuck on the phone for over an hour, repeating my issue to multiple agents, just to retrieve my own account information and unlock access.

What a complete mess of IT systems and customer service. It’s frustrating, time-wasting, and makes basic banking painful. Citi really needs to fix this broken process.

review: I mainly use the citi bank app to deposit my checks and I haven’t seen any problems until the latest update. Today I wanted to deposit my check, added the account I wanted to go, the amount it was for and lastly the pic of the check and here is where the bug appeared. Whenever I tried to go to the banner for the take a photo of my check, it redirects to a full screen message that says “Full screen is required” I’m not listening to anything on the background or watching anything for that matter but it’s just annoying. In previous versions I didn’t have this anomaly but I can replicate it consistently. So it’s no user error.

WHY Did the new  virtual card “update” eliminate expiration dates and the ability to set spending amounts??? Those are what made this feature so great!  And even more importantly, WHY don’t virtual cards even work, WEEKS  after the horrible update? 

If you can’t correctly program this feature, especially after all this time, I no longer trust ANY of your programming… time to pull accounts and go elsewhere.

title: Garbage
review: Citibank is absolutely unsatisfactory. We opened a card and did a balance transfer under the impression that it was zero interest. We then used it for other purchases and got charged interest. When we called to inquire, we learned it was no interest only on the balance transfer, which was fine. We paid the amount down to the balance transfer. However, the next month we were charged interest again. After another call, we discovered that only a portion of the payment was applied to the current amount, with the rest going to the balance transfer. We paid the entire balance in August, but lo and behold, we were charged interest yet again. This company is so terrible and deceitful; I would never recommend using them as they are absolutely unsatisfactory.

I have years worth of emails and this company has ghosted the internet!!!! A Simon search will show that they haven’t interacted with the public in two years!  Do not trust citi. review:
I gave them more than enough time to fix virtual account numbers in the app but at this point it’s just ridiculous.  

Anytime I try to create one or modify a current one I get a big red error at the top saying they’re experiencing issues.  

Also (not app related) but the new rendition of virtual account numbers from website are getting to be less and less useful.  “Daily” limits are now set amounts in large increments as if you’re gonna always know where your electric bill will fall?  So you need to make the limit way higher than it needs to be.  And you can no longer edit the limit.   I liked to put mine at $1 when not using it and raise it to the (exact) amount only when needed.  

So a less useful service that is totally useless from a mobile device, the thing most use more often these days. 

Do better folks.

Had to call and open dispute again, I was told I had to email and the call was useless. I sent 5 separate emails showing all my proof & the policies on the merchants website. In every email I asked them to call me and discuss. I also emailed the merchant again. Finally the merchant initiated the refund 67 days after first request of cancellation. No one from citi has ever called me.

title: Virtual card number broke since Aug 24
review:
Citi updated their virtual card number functionality on Aug 24 (requires new numbers for those saved with a merchant, no longer can specify an expiration date (it’s now fixed at three years), can only limit dollars to four values via a pull down, or unlimited — I dislike the new limitations on date and dollars).

Generating a virtual number on a their web page works, but  the app won’t generate virtual numbers since August 24, and sometimes won’t let you go into Virtual any all, so you can’t see existing virtual numbers or transactions. 

It’s also harder to get to virtual numbers. 

Prior to this I would have given the app five stars.

title: Citi VAN changes are a disaster
review: The virtual account number feature needs to be reverted to it's previous state. The recent changes have added so much red tape and inconvenience as to make it "virtually" unusable. Bring back the old interface in the app, eliminate the need to log in and generate a new CVV for each transaction, and bring back flexible, changeable limits that can't be overridden by merchants! The new VAN screen in the app only displays the numbers in a small lower section of the screen. Terrible UX!

Only 30% of my score. Bravo Citi B. Oh and thanks for raising my apr to 38% (FACTS) what amazing respect for the consumer. Bravo!!
Eric R.

title: Virtual accounts is why I got this card...
review:
...So why make it nonfunctional?
Supposed "improvements" to Virtual accounts were made over the weekend.
Now, you cannot generate a virtual number on the app nor on the desktop site.
Desktop site has even more limited functionality than app. You cannot choose an expiration date (they give you "three years" for the credit card)
You cannot choose dollar limit, only a daily allowance maximum but you can only choose 1 of 5 preset amounts, with maximum of $300.

The Citibank Virtual account program has now become worthless.
Been with them for years, but now I guess it is time to move on to a different credit card altogether.
Very disappointed in the "improvement to virtual accounts".

title: Extremely poor customer service
review: Called customer service to activate my mother’s card because she doesn’t speak English and I had the phone on speaker. The representative, Connie (or something like that), asked to speak with the account holder, my mother, which is standard for security purposes and I understand. I told the representative that my mother did not speak English very well, but she insisted on speaking with her. After asking the general questions (Name, last 4 of social security, address), Connie asked my mother what her security word was, which my mother didn’t know and didn’t understand what it meant. So, my mother said she didn’t know, which Connie didn’t hear/understand, so I explained that my mother didn’t understand and didn’t know what that was, and Connie rudely interrupted me stating she could only speak to the account holder and no one else. This obviously upset me, as I was just trying to help and my mother even apologized in her broken English for not understanding and stated that I was her daughter, trying to convey that it was alright to speak to me, for me to translate for her, which is what I was already there for. In total, it took over 20 minutes for Connie to get a translator, confirm all the security answers and my mother’s identity, and activate the card because Connie did not want me to translate and the interpreter got some information wrong. The card could have been activated in 5 minutes give or take, if she had just let me translate, but noooo. And chat support is slow as well, taking 20 minutes to respond.

title: It’s a great app to protect yourself when your card is lost and more
review:
I use the Citibank app all the time. If anyone in my family can’t find their card, I lock it immediately. And during that time. I can open the card up for a minute if someone else needs to use it with their card.
I also use the feature for alerts. I have Citibank send notification for every transaction to my text message. So I have actively caught a few scandals. And immediately locked my card to prevent more illegal transactions.

The only issue I have with Citibank is there Fraud Department, when you call for an emergency, you get caught up online or going through prompts. Often, I am driving hands-free. Plus, it takes way too long to get to a person. And since we navigate most of our support ourselves, they should have one line with a direct dial to fraud department.

Their policies are ridiculous, as they hold onto your funds and won’t let you have access to them much longer than any other bank I have dealt with in the past. The lack of consideration and service that their supervisor provided was horrible and the bank’s policies are definitely not in favor of their customers needs!  If you’re looking for a financial institution to use for your business or personal finances, I recommend that you consider any other bank besides Citibank. title: Frustrating Experience with Citibank Customer Service
review:
I typically don’t have issues with Citibank—until I actually need help with something important. When I recently needed assistance, I encountered a frustrating experience. There seemed to be a language barrier with the staff, and rather than working to understand and resolve my issue, they were mostly silent and unhelpful.

Instead of addressing the problem, I was redirected back to the same customer service number I originally called, forcing me to start the entire process over again. It felt like the staff was more trained in saying they can’t help than actually working toward a solution. This cycle of being passed around with no real answers felt designed to frustrate you into giving up.

I’m sharing this review so others are aware of what they might face when seeking support from Citibank. Hopefully, it encourages the company to improve the effectiveness of its customer service team.
